title: thymus serpyllum var. montanus
intro: Thymus serpyllum var. montanus, also known as Mountain Thyme or Alpine Thyme, is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family and native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Its medicinal properties include its ability to soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing symptoms of indigestion and nausea, while its essential oils have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and antiseptic properties. Herbal preparations of Mountain Thyme can be consumed as teas, infusions, or tinctures, making it a popular natural remedy for promoting overall health and well-being.
